title: Validated text input deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Added digest-pinned deterministic executable evidence for Validated text input; AI-assisted draft remains human-unreviewed and exam-ineligible.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Validated text input: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? scanf with %s or %d always writes a usable value, so its return result and destination width can be ignored.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Check the input function's return value before reading destinations and provide explicit bounds such as fgets capacity or a width-limited string conversion.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Check the input function's return value before reading destinations and provide explicit bounds such as fgets capacity or a width-limited string conversion.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

items.title: Validated text input: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing C checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: scanf with %s or %d always writes a usable value, so its return result and destination width can be ignored.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'return value'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. Check the input function's return value before reading destinations and provide explicit bounds such as fgets capacity or a width-limited string conversion.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact C token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.

items.template: Check the input function's {{key-rule}} before reading destinations and provide explicit bounds such as fgets capacity or a width-limited string conversion.

items.title: Validated text input: executable application
items.prompt: Read one bounded text line, parse exactly one base-10 integer from 0 through 100, and print the value or invalid without using scanf for the conversion.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: bounded line input and complete strtol validation

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises bounded line input and complete strtol validation under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
